2603.20907.title: The Hidden Puppet Master: A Theoretical and Real-World Account of Emotional Manipulation in LLMs
2603.20907.paper_summary: As users increasingly turn to LLMs for practical and personal advice, they become vulnerable to being subtly steered toward hidden incentives misaligned with their own interests. Prior works have benchmarked persuasion and manipulation detection, but these efforts rely on simulated or debate-style settings, remain uncorrelated with real human belief shifts, and overlook a critical dimension: the morality of hidden incentives driving the manipulation. We introduce PUPPET, a theoretical taxonomy of personalized emotional manipulation in LLM-human dialogues that centers around incentive morality, and conduct a human study with N=1,035 participants across realistic everyday queries, varying personalization and incentive direction (harmful versus prosocial). We find that harmful hidden incentives produce significantly larger belief shifts than prosocial ones. Finally, we benchmark LLMs on the task of belief prediction, finding that models exhibit moderate predictive ability of belief change based on conversational contexts (r=0.3 - 0.5), but they also systematically underestimate the magnitude of belief shift. Together, this work establishes a theoretically grounded and behaviorally validated foundation for studying, and ultimately combatting, incentive-driven manipulation in LLMs during everyday, practical user queries.
2603.20907.paper_summary_zh: 隨著用戶越來越多地轉向大型語言模型（LLMs）尋求實用和個人建議，他們變得容易受到微妙的引導，朝向與自身利益不一致的隱性激勵。先前的研究已經對說服和操控檢測進行了基準測試，但這些努力依賴於模擬或辯論風格的情境，與真實的人類信念變化無關，並忽視了一個關鍵維度：驅動操控的隱性激勵的道德性。我們介紹了PUPPET，這是一個圍繞激勵道德的個性化情感操控的理論分類法，並進行了一項人類研究，參與者數量為1,035，涵蓋了現實日常查詢，變化的個性化和激勵方向（有害與利社會）。我們發現，有害的隱性激勵產生的信念變化顯著大於利社會的激勵。最後，我們在信念預測的任務上對LLMs進行基準測試，發現模型在根據對話上下文預測信念變化方面展現出中等的預測能力（r=0.3 - 0.5），但它們也系統性地低估了信念變化的幅度。綜合來看，這項工作為研究和最終對抗LLMs在日常實用用戶查詢中驅動的激勵操控建立了一個理論基礎和行為驗證的基礎。
2603.20907.author: Jocelyn Shen et.al.
2603.20907.authors: Jocelyn Shen, Amina Luvsanchultem, Jessica Kim, Kynnedy Smith, Valdemar Danry, Kantwon Rogers, Sharifa Alghowinem, Hae Won Park, Maarten Sap, Cynthia Breazeal
